content:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: I will own this file since only one owner is allowed. Please send a SPEC for your tip_weights feature (init param name/default, _select_tip_with_weights signature/behavior, and exact change to the tip-selection block in propose_instructions_for_program) and I will implement it alongside my cache-salt changes.

content:
type: DEFER
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
spec: Implement the following in dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (in addition to your own tip_weights feature):

1) Remove temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor:
   - Delete these lines entirely:
       original_temp = self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"]
       epsilon = self.rng.uniform(0.01, 0.05)
       modified_temp = T + epsilon
     and the lines:
       self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"] = modified_temp
       ...
       self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"] = original_temp
   - Keep `with dspy.settings.context(lm=self.prompt_model):` wrapping the instruction_generator(...) call (just drop the temperature kwarg mutation lines inside/around it).
   - Keep the `T` parameter in propose_instruction_for_predictor's and propose_instructions_for_program's signatures for API compatibility, but it's now unused inside propose_instruction_for_predictor. Add a comment on the `T,  # noqa: N803` line in propose_instruction_for_predictor: `# kept for API compatibility; no kwarg modulation`.

2) Add this exact private method to the GroundedProposer class (same level as propose_instructions_for_program / propose_instruction_for_predictor):

   def _cache_salt(self, pred_i: int, demo_set_i: int) -> str:
       # Deterministic given self.rng (seeded upstream) + stable IDs
       if hasattr(self.rng, "getrandbits"):
           n = self.rng.getrandbits(64) ^ ((pred_i & 0xFFFF) << 16) ^ (demo_set_i & 0xFFFF)
       else:
           # Deterministic fallback when self.rng has no getrandbits (e.g. a mock)
           base = ((pred_i & 0xFFFFFFFF) << 32) ^ (demo_set_i & 0xFFFFFFFF)
           n = base ^ 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15
           n &= (1 << 64) - 1
       # Base32 (no padding) for compact ASCII salt
       alphabet = \"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ234567\"
       out = []
       x = n
       for _ in range(13):  # 13*5=65 bits coverage
           out.append(alphabet[x & 31]); x >>= 5
       return \"\".join(out)

   Result must be exactly 13 ASCII chars from that alphabet, deterministic w.r.t. self.rng + (pred_i, demo_set_i).

3) In propose_instruction_for_predictor, compute once before calling instruction_generator:
       cache_salt = self._cache_salt(pred_i=pred_i, demo_set_i=demo_set_i)
   and pass it into the instruction_generator(...) call:
       proposed_instruction = instruction_generator(
           demo_candidates=demo_candidates,
           pred_i=pred_i,
           demo_set_i=demo_set_i,
           program=program,
           data_summary=self.data_summary,
           previous_instructions=instruction_history,
           num_demos_in_context=self.num_demos_in_context,
           tip=tip,
           cache_salt=cache_salt,
       ).proposed_instruction

4) Add a new dspy.InputField at the END of these three signature classes (same file):
   - DescribeProgram
   - DescribeModule
   - GenerateSingleModuleInstruction (defined inside generate_instruction_class(...))
   Exact field:
       cache_salt = dspy.InputField(
           format=str,
           desc=\"For caching only. Ignored by the model.\",
           prefix=\"CACHE SALT (IGNORE):\",
       )

5) In GenerateModuleInstruction.forward(...):
   - Add a new parameter `cache_salt=None` to the forward signature.
   - Pass cache_salt=cache_salt to self.describe_program(...) and self.describe_module(...) calls (when self.program_aware branch runs), and to self.generate_module_instruction(...) call. Same salt value reused for all three calls within one forward invocation (it's just threaded through from the parameter, not recomputed).

No other behavioral changes needed from me. Thanks for owning this file.

1) Remove temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor:

2) Add this exact private method to the GroundedProposer class (same level as propose_instructions_for_program / propose_instruction_for_predictor):

3) In propose_instruction_for_predictor, compute once before calling instruction_generator:
       cache_salt = self._cache_salt(pred_i=pred_i, demo_set_i=demo_set_i)
   and pass it into the instruction_generator(...) call:

           demo_candidates=demo_candidates,

           previous_instructions=instruction_history,
           num_demos_in_context=self.num_demos_in_context,

       ).proposed_instruction

4) Add a new dspy.InputField at the END of these three signature classes (same file):
   - DescribeProgram
   - DescribeModule
   - GenerateSingleModuleInstruction (defined inside generate_instruction_class(...))
   Exact field:
       cache_salt = dspy.InputField(
           format=str,
           desc=\"For caching only. Ignored by the model.\",
           prefix=\"CACHE SALT (IGNORE):\",
       )

5) In GenerateModuleInstruction.forward(...):
   - Add a new parameter `cache_salt=None` to the forward signature.
   - Pass cache_salt=cache_salt to self.describe_program(...) and self.describe_module(...) calls (when self.program_aware branch runs), and to self.generate_module_instruction(...) call. Same salt value reused for all three calls within one forward invocation (it's just threaded through from the parameter, not recomputed).

No other behavioral changes needed from me. Thanks for owning this file.

content:
type: DONE
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Implemented both cache_salt/temperature-removal (my feature) and tip_weights/_select_tip_with_weights (your spec) in grounded_proposer.py. All existing tests pass (tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py, tests/propose/), ruff clean. Wrote diff to patch.txt.
